items.title: Titans, DT Simmons agree to $105M+ extension
items.body_text: The Titans and defensive tackle Jeffery Simmons have agreed to a three-year, $105.8M extension, his agents told ESPN's Adam Schefter and NFL Network's Ian Rapoport and Mike Garafolo.
items.author: Turron Davenport

items.title: Source: Big 12 monitoring Sorsby fallout, options
items.body_text: The Big 12 presidents and chancellors haven't rushed to pull the league's recently filed federal lawsuit because there are still questions about Texas Tech quarterback Brendan Sorsby's NFL future and any possible legal retribution, a source told ESPN.
items.author: Heather Dinich

items.title: Fresher than listed: Sauce says he's 24, not 25
items.body_text: Colts cornerback Sauce Gardner says he is 24 years old, not 25 as he is listed in many corners of the internet.
items.author: Stephen Holder

items.title: ESPN: Pennel knew woman whose body found
items.body_text: Michael Pennel Jr. had an ongoing relationship with a young woman whose body was found on a property he owned when she went missing, an ESPN investigation found, contradicting Pennel's claim he did not know her.
items.author: T.J. Quinn and Juan Recio

items.title: Burrow: Bengals have same vibes as LSU title team
items.body_text: Joe Burrow said the vibes on this year's Bengals' team are similar to what they were on the 2019 LSU team he led to a College Football Playoff championship.
items.author: Ben Baby

items.title: Daniels still adjusting to new Washington offense
items.body_text: Commanders quarterback Jayden Daniels, trying to rebound from a tough injury-filled season, said he likes their new offense -- but a lot more work remains.
items.author: John Keim

items.title: Cards QB Beck trying to speed up learning curve
items.body_text: Cardinals quarterback Carson Beck said he's jumped in headfirst into the early days of his NFL career rather than trying to take things slow.
items.author: Josh Weinfuss

items.title: Bowles confirms Bucs' Vea a minicamp hold-in
items.body_text: Bucs defensive tackle Vita Vea is entering the final year of his $71 million contract, without guaranteed money, and is not physically participating in practice.
items.author: Jenna Laine

items.title: 👀 Broncos may have started a salary cap trend
items.body_text: The Broncos took a then-record $85 million dead money hit by parting with Wilson in 2024, but their quick turnaround has turned heads.
